commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=6896d3b2ab6d35e128d1ec6e1b46dfeca432326c)
commands.review_summary:
The branch moves voice-call realtime barge-in cancellation from local MuLaw speech detection to provider `input_audio_buffer.speech_started` events, guards cancellation on active assistant audio, and updates the realtime handler test.

PR surface: Source +4, Tests +13. Total +17 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: current main cancels on local MuLaw speech detection, while the PR version only logs local detection. Docs and Google provider code show Google is a supported realtime voice provider that does not emit the OpenAI `input_audio_buffer.speech_started` event.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review is needed because the branch needs contributor real behavior proof and a compatibility fix for provider-agnostic barge-in before any repair or merge lane should proceed.

target.title: Fix realtime voice-call barge-in cancellation
target.body:
## Summary

- Move realtime voice-call barge-in cancellation from local MuLaw speech detection to the provider VAD `input_audio_buffer.speech_started` event.
- Guard cancellation with `talk.outputAudioActive` so user speech only cancels an active assistant audio response.
- Keep local MuLaw speech detection as diagnostic logging while still forwarding input audio deltas.

## Tests

- `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/voice-call/src/webhook/realtime-handler.test.ts -t "barge-in cancellation"`
- `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/voice-call/src/webhook/realtime-handler.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

<!-- agent-transcript:start -->
## Agent Transcript

<details>
<summary>Redacted OpenClaw agent session transcript</summary>

```text
source: local OpenClaw session logs
redaction: local paths, phone/account identifiers, auth details, raw tool outputs, and unrelated routing metadata omitted
scope: fix/realtime-provider-barge-in; extensions/voice-call/src/webhook/realtime-handler.ts; extensions/voice-call/src/webhook/realtime-handler.test.ts

[user]
Asked to turn the realtime voice-call barge-in fix into a PR-ready change.

[assistant]
Checked the working tree, focused the PR scope on the voice-call realtime handler, and verified the existing partial change only removed local MuLaw-triggered cancellation.

[assistant]
Identified the missing production behavior: provider VAD event `input_audio_buffer.speech_started` needed to cancel the active assistant output turn, while local MuLaw speech detection should remain diagnostic only.

[tool summary]
Updated `extensions/voice-call/src/webhook/realtime-handler.ts` so provider `speech_started` clears queued outbound audio and cancels the active Talk turn only when `talk.outputAudioActive` is true.

[tool summary]
Updated `extensions/voice-call/src/webhook/realtime-handler.test.ts` so the barge-in cancellation test drives provider `input_audio_buffer.speech_started` instead of local media chunks.

[assistant]
Verified the targeted barge-in test passed, then ran the full `realtime-handler.test.ts` file successfully.

[tool summary]
Proof captured: targeted Vitest passed, full realtime handler Vitest passed, and `git diff --check` reported no whitespace errors.
```

</details>
<!-- agent-transcript:end -->
target.author: moellenbeck
